mappings.rationaleAI: The Inca Empire's administrative structure, road network, khipu records, agricultural terracing, sun worship, and Spanish conquest directly address the empire of the Incas (C), including origins and development through conquest and civil war (C.1) and government, society, and culture (C.2).

Andean civilizations from Norte Chico through Chavin, Nazca, Moche, Wari, Tiwanaku, Chimu, and Inca provide comprehensive coverage of the character of Andean civilization (A), pre-Inca cultures (B), and the Inca empire (C).
The Spanish conquest of the Inca Empire directly addresses the Spanish conquest of the Incas (1532-40) including Pizarro's execution of Atahuallpa (C.1.f).
South America's geography including the Andes and prehistory of Andean civilizations provides context for the character and geography of Andean civilization (A).
South American history's coverage of pre-Columbian civilizations including Norte Chico/Caral-Supe, Chavin, Moche, and Inca provides broad context across all major outline sections.
The Spanish Empire's conquest of the Inca Empire and establishment of colonial rule directly addresses the Spanish conquest of the Incas (C.1.f).
The pre-Columbian era's coverage of Andean civilizations including Chavin, Moche, Wari, Tiwanaku, and Inca provides comprehensive context across all outline sections.
The Andes mountain range's geology, climate, and indigenous agricultural traditions provide geographic context for the character and geography of Andean civilization (A).
The potato's domestication in the Andes 7,000-10,000 years ago relates to the Late Preceramic period's development of agriculture (B.1).
